UADataType.Description: Selector mode of the generator set control panel.

Field.Name: Off
Field.Description: Control is off; the set will not start automatically or manually.

Field.Name: Manual
Field.Description: Manual/hand mode; the set runs on operator command.

Field.Name: Auto
Field.Description: Automatic mode; the set starts/stops on remote or utility-failure signals.

Field.Name: Test
Field.Description: Test mode; a commanded test run, optionally with load.

Field.Name: Exercise
Field.Description: Scheduled exercise/self-test run.

Field.Name: RemoteStart
Field.Description: Started by a remote start signal.

Field.Name: Maintenance
Field.Description: Maintenance/service mode; starting is inhibited or restricted.

Field.Name: Lockout
Field.Description: Locked out; starting is blocked until reset.

UADataType.Description: Primary fuel of the generator set.
Application/duty rating per ISO 8528 plus the data-center-continuous rating.

Field.Name: EmergencyStandby
Field.Description: ESP: standby power at variable load, limited hours, no overload.

Field.Name: Prime
Field.Description: PRP: unlimited hours at variable load, typically 10% overload 1h/12h.

Field.Name: Continuous
Field.Description: COP: unlimited hours at constant load, no overload.

Field.Name: LimitedTime
Field.Description: LTP: limited hours per year at defined load.

Field.Name: DataCenterContinuous
Field.Description: DCC: continuous operation for data-center loads.

UADataType.Description: Winding/connection configuration of the alternator output.

Field.Name: PMG
Field.Description: Permanent Magnet Generator - independent excitation supply.

UADataType.Description: Primary cooling method of the engine.
Air induction method of the engine.
Emissions certification standard of the engine.
State of the engine CAN bus / SAE J1939 network interface.
Contact position of an automatic transfer switch.

Field.Name: Source1
Field.Description: Connected to Source 1 (normal/utility).

Field.Name: Source2
Field.Description: Connected to Source 2 (emergency/generator).

Field.Name: Neutral
Field.Description: Center-off / neutral position.

UADataType.Description: Transition method of an automatic transfer switch.

Field.Name: OpenTransition
Field.Description: Break-before-make.

Field.Name: DelayedTransition
Field.Description: Break-before-make with center-off delay.

Field.Name: ClosedTransition
Field.Description: Make-before-break; momentary paralleling.

Field.Name: SoftLoadTransition
Field.Description: Ramped, no-break transfer while paralleled.

UADataType.Description: Operating state of an automatic transfer switch.
Severity class of a generator protection event.

Field.Name: Derate
Field.Description: The set continues to run at reduced output.

Field.Name: Shutdown
Field.Description: The engine is shut down.

Field.Name: ElectricalTrip
Field.Description: The generator breaker is tripped.

Field.Name: Lockout
Field.Description: The set is locked out and requires manual reset.

UADataType.Description: Protection / fault function that raised a generator alarm.

Field.Name: Overcrank
Field.Description: Fail to start within the crank limit.

UADataType.Description: Operating state of a paralleling / switchgear system.
State of the exhaust aftertreatment system.
SAE J1939 DM1 diagnostic lamp status (lamp state plus flash rate).

Field.Name: Off
Field.Description: The lamp is off.

Field.Name: On
Field.Description: The lamp is on (steady).

Field.Name: SlowFlash
Field.Description: The lamp is flashing slowly.

Field.Name: FastFlash
Field.Description: The lamp is flashing fast.

Field.Name: NotAvailable
Field.Description: The lamp status is not available.

UADataType.Description: A SAE J1939 diagnostic trouble code (DTC) reported by an engine ECU.

Field.Name: Spn
Field.Description: Suspect Parameter Number identifying the faulty subsystem.

Field.Name: Fmi
Field.Description: Failure Mode Identifier describing the type of failure.

Field.Name: OccurrenceCount
Field.Description: Number of times the fault has become active.

Field.Name: ConversionMethod
Field.Description: J1939 SPN conversion method flag.

Field.Name: Active
Field.Description: TRUE while the fault is currently active (DM1).

Field.Name: SourceAddress
Field.Description: J1939 source address of the ECU that reported the code.

Field.Name: SourceName
Field.Description: Name of the ECU/controller that reported the code.

Field.Name: Severity
Field.Description: Severity classification of the fault.

Field.Name: Description
Field.Description: Human-readable description of the fault.

UAObjectType.Description: Identification and nameplate of a generator set. Extends the Machinery MachineIdentificationType with generator-specific nameplate data.

UAVariable.Description: Vendor build/specification code that identifies the exact configuration.
Vendor product family or series to which the set belongs.
Model designation of the prime-mover engine.
Serial number of the prime-mover engine.
Model designation of the alternator.
Serial number of the alternator.
Model designation of the control panel.
Primary fuel of the set.
Emissions certification standard.
Nameplate rated real power. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Nameplate rated apparent power. EngineeringUnits: kVA.
Nameplate rated line-to-line voltage. EngineeringUnits: V.
Nameplate rated frequency. EngineeringUnits: Hz.
Sound pressure level at 7 m (23 ft) in dB(A).

UAObjectType.Description: Finite state machine describing the operating state of a generator set: Off, Ready, Starting, Warmup, Running, Loaded, Synchronizing, Paralleled, Cooldown, Stopping, Fault and EmergencyStopped.

UAObject.Description: State 'Off' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Ready' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Starting' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Warmup' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Running' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Loaded' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Synchronizing' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Paralleled' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Cooldown' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Stopping' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'Fault' of the generator set operating state machine.
State 'EmergencyStopped' of the generator set operating state machine.

UAObjectType.Description: The engine CAN bus / SAE J1939 diagnostic interface. Surfaces the network connection parameters, J1939 lamp status and active/previously-active diagnostic trouble codes reported by the engine ECU.

UAVariable.Description: Name of the diagnostic protocol, e.g. 'SAE J1939'.
Name/identifier of the CAN network (e.g. CAN0).
J1939 source address of the engine ECU.
CAN bit rate in bit/s (typically 250000 or 500000).
State of the CAN bus interface.
J1939 DM1 amber warning lamp status.
J1939 DM1 red stop lamp status.
J1939 DM1 malfunction indicator lamp status.
J1939 DM1 protect lamp status.
Currently active DTCs (J1939 DM1).
Previously active DTCs (J1939 DM2).

UAMethod.Description: Clear previously active diagnostic trouble codes (J1939 DM3/DM11).

UAObjectType.Description: Exhaust aftertreatment subsystem (DPF/SCR/DEF) for Tier 4 / Stage V engines.

UAVariable.Description: State of the aftertreatment system.
Diesel Exhaust Fluid tank level. SAE J1939 SPN 1761. EngineeringUnits: %.
DEF tank temperature. SAE J1939 SPN 3031. EngineeringUnits: degC.
DEF concentration/quality. SAE J1939 SPN 3364. EngineeringUnits: %.
Diesel particulate filter soot load. SAE J1939 SPN 3719. EngineeringUnits: %.
Diesel particulate filter ash load. SAE J1939 SPN 3720. EngineeringUnits: %.
Exhaust gas temperature. SAE J1939 SPN 173. EngineeringUnits: degC.
A DPF regeneration is required.
DPF regeneration is currently inhibited.

UAMethod.Description: Enable or disable the inhibit of automatic regeneration.

UAObjectType.Description: The prime-mover engine of a generator set. Exposes engine telemetry, typically obtained over the CAN bus / SAE J1939 interface, and identification.

UAVariable.Description: Engine speed. SAE J1939 SPN 190. EngineeringUnits: rpm.
Engine percent load at current speed. SAE J1939 SPN 92. EngineeringUnits: %.
Actual engine percent torque. SAE J1939 SPN 513. EngineeringUnits: %.
Engine oil pressure. SAE J1939 SPN 100. EngineeringUnits: kPa.
Engine oil temperature. SAE J1939 SPN 175. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Engine coolant temperature. SAE J1939 SPN 110. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Engine coolant pressure. SAE J1939 SPN 109. EngineeringUnits: kPa.
Engine fuel consumption rate. SAE J1939 SPN 183. EngineeringUnits: L/h.
Engine fuel temperature. SAE J1939 SPN 174. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Intake manifold (boost) pressure. SAE J1939 SPN 102. EngineeringUnits: kPa.
Intake manifold temperature. SAE J1939 SPN 105. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Exhaust gas temperature. SAE J1939 SPN 173. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Ambient barometric pressure. SAE J1939 SPN 108. EngineeringUnits: kPa.
Total engine run hours. SAE J1939 SPN 247. EngineeringUnits: h.
Total number of engine start attempts.
Air induction method of the engine.
Engine displacement. EngineeringUnits: l.
Number of cylinders.
Rated (synchronous) engine speed, e.g. 1500 or 1800 rpm. EngineeringUnits: rpm.

UAObject.Description: CAN bus / SAE J1939 diagnostic interface of the engine ECU.

UAObjectType.Description: Per-phase electrical measurements of the alternator output.

UAVariable.Description: Phase (line-to-neutral) RMS voltage. EngineeringUnits: V.
Line-to-line RMS voltage referenced to the next phase. EngineeringUnits: V.
Phase RMS current. EngineeringUnits: A.
Per-phase real power. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Per-phase reactive power. EngineeringUnits: kvar.
Per-phase apparent power. EngineeringUnits: kVA.
Per-phase power factor (-1..1).

UAObjectType.Description: The alternator (generator end) that converts mechanical power into AC electrical power. Exposes aggregate and per-phase electrical measurements.

UAVariable.Description: Output frequency. EngineeringUnits: Hz.
Average line-to-line RMS voltage. EngineeringUnits: V.
Average line-to-neutral RMS voltage. EngineeringUnits: V.
Average line RMS current. EngineeringUnits: A.
Total three-phase real power. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Total three-phase reactive power. EngineeringUnits: kvar.
Total three-phase apparent power. EngineeringUnits: kVA.
Average power factor (-1..1).
Cumulative generated real energy. EngineeringUnits: kW.h.
Output as a percentage of rated power. EngineeringUnits: %.
Stator winding temperature, phase 1. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Stator winding temperature, phase 2. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Stator winding temperature, phase 3. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Drive-end bearing temperature. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Non-drive-end bearing temperature. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Winding connection configuration.
Excitation method.
Number of alternator poles.
AVR voltage setpoint. EngineeringUnits: V.
Excitation field current. EngineeringUnits: A.

UAObjectType.Description: The fuel storage and delivery subsystem of a generator set.

UAVariable.Description: Primary fuel of the set.
Fuel tank level. EngineeringUnits: %.
Usable fuel volume remaining. EngineeringUnits: l.
Fuel consumption rate. EngineeringUnits: L/h.
Fuel supply pressure. EngineeringUnits: kPa.
Fuel temperature. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Gas inlet pressure for gaseous-fuel sets. EngineeringUnits: kPa.
Estimated runtime remaining at current load. EngineeringUnits: h.
Cumulative fuel consumed. EngineeringUnits: l.
Water detected in the fuel/water separator.

UAObjectType.Description: The engine cooling subsystem of a generator set.

UAVariable.Description: Engine coolant temperature. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Coolant level. SAE J1939 SPN 111. EngineeringUnits: %.
Coolant pressure. EngineeringUnits: kPa.
Cooling method (air- or liquid-cooled).
Ambient air temperature at the set. EngineeringUnits: degC.
The radiator fan is running.
The jacket-water block heater is active.

UAObjectType.Description: The engine lubrication subsystem of a generator set.

UAVariable.Description: Engine oil pressure. SAE J1939 SPN 100. EngineeringUnits: kPa.
Engine oil temperature. SAE J1939 SPN 175. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Engine oil level. EngineeringUnits: %.
Oil filter differential pressure. EngineeringUnits: kPa.

UAObjectType.Description: The starting/battery subsystem of a generator set.

UAVariable.Description: Starting battery voltage. SAE J1939 SPN 168. EngineeringUnits: V.
Battery charging current. EngineeringUnits: A.
The battery charger is active.
Number of start attempts in the last start sequence.

UAObjectType.Description: The generator set control panel. Provides controller identity, mode/state visibility and remote-monitoring status.

UAVariable.Description: Vendor controller product family or product line.
Controller firmware version.
Application software version.
Configuration/calibration version.
The controller is in automatic mode.
The controller is NOT in automatic mode (NFPA annunciation).
Remote start is enabled.
Remote control is enabled.
Connected to the remote-monitoring cloud.
The Modbus interface is enabled.
Cellular/network signal strength. EngineeringUnits: %.

UAObjectType.Description: A single nameplate power rating point of a generator set for a given application/duty (ISO 8528).

UAVariable.Description: Application/duty of this rating.
Rated real power. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Rated apparent power. EngineeringUnits: kVA.
Rated power factor.
Rated line-to-line voltage. EngineeringUnits: V.
Rated line current. EngineeringUnits: A.
Rated frequency. EngineeringUnits: Hz.
Rated engine speed. EngineeringUnits: rpm.
Number of phases (1 or 3).
Winding connection for this rating.
Reference ambient temperature for the rating. EngineeringUnits: degC.
Reference altitude for the rating. EngineeringUnits: m.

UAObjectType.Description: Alarm raised by a generator protection/shutdown function. Extends OffNormalAlarmType with the protection function, severity and J1939 origin.

UAVariable.Description: The protection function that raised the alarm.
Severity class of the alarm.
TRUE if the condition caused an engine shutdown.
SAE J1939 SPN when the alarm originates from the engine ECU.
SAE J1939 FMI when the alarm originates from the engine ECU.
Name of the originating subsystem.

UAObjectType.Description: A generator set (GenSet): a complete electrical power generation asset composed of a prime-mover engine, an alternator, a fuel system, cooling, lubrication, starting and control subsystems. Applicable to the whole industry, from small home-standby units to house-sized multi-megawatt industrial sets.

UAObject.Description: Generator identification and nameplate (Machinery building block).
Container for standardized Machinery building blocks.

UAVariable.Description: Selector mode of the control panel (Off/Manual/Auto/Test/...).
Emissions certification standard of the set.
Application segment, e.g. Residential, DataCenter, Healthcare, Rental, PrimePower.
The generator (output) breaker is closed.
The generator breaker is available to close.
The remote start input is asserted.
A run request is active from any source.
Loading of the set is inhibited.
The set is up to speed and voltage and is ready to accept load.

UAObject.Description: Nameplate power ratings of the set (e.g. Standby and Prime).
A nameplate rating point of the set.

UAMethod.Description: Command the set to start in the current operating mode.
Start a test run for a given duration.

Description.Text: TRUE to run the test with load.

UAObjectType.Description: One power source (normal/utility or emergency/generator) of an automatic transfer switch, with its availability and measurements.

UAVariable.Description: The source is present and energized.
The source is within acceptable voltage/frequency limits.
Source line-to-line voltage. EngineeringUnits: V.
Source frequency. EngineeringUnits: Hz.
Phase rotation of the source (e.g. ABC or CBA).

UAObjectType.Description: An automatic transfer switch (ATS) that transfers a load between a normal source (utility) and an emergency source (generator).

UAVariable.Description: Contact position of the switch.
Operating state of the switch.
Transition method of the switch.
The preferred source (1 = normal, 2 = emergency).
The load is connected to Source 1.
The load is connected to Source 2.
Transfer is currently inhibited.
Reason transfer is inhibited, if any.
Rated current of the switch. EngineeringUnits: A.
Number of poles.
The switch is service-entrance rated.
Load current through the switch. EngineeringUnits: A.
Cumulative number of transfers.
Timestamp of the last transfer.
Engine-start (outage confirmation) delay.
Delay before transferring to emergency.
Delay before retransferring to normal.
Engine cooldown (unloaded run) delay.

UAMethod.Description: Command a transfer to the emergency source.
Command a retransfer to the normal source.
Enable or disable the transfer inhibit.

UAObjectType.Description: A paralleling / switchgear controller that synchronizes and shares load among generator sets on a common bus, and optionally parallels with the utility.

UAVariable.Description: Operating state of the paralleling system.
Common bus voltage. EngineeringUnits: V.
Common bus frequency. EngineeringUnits: Hz.
Total real power on the bus. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Total reactive power on the bus. EngineeringUnits: kvar.
Phase angle difference during synchronizing. EngineeringUnits: deg.
Slip frequency during synchronizing. EngineeringUnits: Hz.
Voltage difference during synchronizing. EngineeringUnits: V.
Frequency difference during synchronizing. EngineeringUnits: Hz.
Synchronism-check permissive to close.
The bus is dead (de-energized).
This set's share of the total bus load. EngineeringUnits: %.
Available spare capacity on the bus. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Spinning reserve on the bus. EngineeringUnits: kW.
The generator breaker is closed.
The utility breaker is closed.
Power imported from the utility. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Power exported to the utility. EngineeringUnits: kW.

UAMethod.Description: Synchronize and close onto the common bus.
Soft-unload and open from the common bus.

UAObjectType.Description: A power-generation system aggregating one or more paralleled generator sets, an optional paralleling controller and transfer switches. Models integrated power systems such as data-center and healthcare plants.

UAObject.Description: The generator sets that make up the system.
A generator set participating in the system.
The paralleling/switchgear controller of the system.
The transfer switches in the system.
A transfer switch in the system.

UAVariable.Description: Number of generator sets in the system.
Total installed capacity of the system. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Total load currently served by the system. EngineeringUnits: kW.
Redundancy scheme, e.g. N, N+1, N+2, 2N, DistributedRedundant.
